[package]
name = "web-tf24a"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
authors = ["Michael A Wright"]
license = "MIT"
description = "Browser-based Forth debugger on COR24"

[lib]
crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]

[dependencies]
cor24-emulator = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs", default-features = false }
cor24-isa = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs/isa", features = ["serde"] }
yew = { version = "0.21", features = ["csr"] }
wasm-bindgen = "0.2"
web-sys = { version = "0.3", features = ["console", "HtmlTextAreaElement", "HtmlInputElement", "HtmlSelectElement", "HtmlElement", "KeyboardEvent"] }
gloo = "0.11"
console_error_panic_hook = "0.1"

[profile.release]
opt-level = "z"
lto = true

1→//! COR24 instruction opcodes and formats.
     2→
     3→/// COR24 instruction opcodes (5-bit, from decoded ROM)
     4→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
     5→#[cfg_attr(feature = "serde", derive(serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize))]
     6→#[repr(u8)]
     7→pub enum Opcode {
     8→    AddReg = 0x00, // add ra,rb
     9→    AddImm = 0x01, // add ra,dd
    10→    And = 0x02,    // and ra,rb
    11→    Bra = 0x03,    // bra dd
    12→    Brf = 0x04,    // brf dd
    13→    Brt = 0x05,    // brt dd
    14→    Ceq = 0x06,    // ceq ra,rb
    15→    Cls = 0x07,    // cls ra,rb (compare less signed)
    16→    Clu = 0x08,    // clu ra,rb (compare less unsigned)
    17→    Jal = 0x09,    // jal ra,(rb)
    18→    Jmp = 0x0A,    // jmp (ra)
    19→    La = 0x0B,     // la ra,dddddd
    20→    Lb = 0x0C,     // lb ra,dd(rb) (signed)
    21→    Lbu = 0x0D,    // lbu ra,dd(rb) (unsigned)
    22→    Lc = 0x0E,     // lc ra,dd (signed)
    23→    Lcu = 0x0F,    // lcu ra,dd (unsigned)
    24→    Lw = 0x10,     // lw ra,dd(rb)
    25→    Mov = 0x11,    // mov ra,rb or mov ra,c
    26→    Mul = 0x12,    // mul ra,rb
    27→    Or = 0x13,     // or ra,rb
    28→    Pop = 0x14,    // pop ra
    29→    Push = 0x15,   // push ra
    30→    Sb = 0x16,     // sb ra,dd(rb)
    31→    Shl = 0x17,    // shl ra,rb
    32→    Sra = 0x18,    // sra ra,rb
    33→    Srl = 0x19,    // srl ra,rb
    34→    Sub = 0x1A,    // sub ra,rb
    35→    SubSp = 0x1B,  // sub sp,dddddd
    36→    Sw = 0x1C,     // sw ra,dd(rb)
    37→    Sxt = 0x1D,    // sxt ra,rb
    38→    Xor = 0x1E,    // xor ra,rb
    39→    Zxt = 0x1F,    // zxt ra,rb
    40→    Invalid = 0xFF,
    41→}
    42→
    43→impl From<u8> for Opcode {
    44→    fn from(value: u8) -> Self {
    45→        match value {
    46→            0x00 => Opcode::AddReg,
    47→            0x01 => Opcode::AddImm,
    48→            0x02 => Opcode::And,
    49→            0x03 => Opcode::Bra,
    50→            0x04 => Opcode::Brf,
    51→            0x05 => Opcode::Brt,
    52→            0x06 => Opcode::Ceq,
    53→            0x07 => Opcode::Cls,
    54→            0x08 => Opcode::Clu,
    55→            0x09 => Opcode::Jal,
    56→            0x0A => Opcode::Jmp,
    57→            0x0B => Opcode::La,
    58→            0x0C => Opcode::Lb,
    59→            0x0D => Opcode::Lbu,
    60→            0x0E => Opcode::Lc,
    61→            0x0F => Opcode::Lcu,
    62→            0x10 => Opcode::Lw,
    63→            0x11 => Opcode::Mov,
    64→            0x12 => Opcode::Mul,
    65→            0x13 => Opcode::Or,
    66→            0x14 => Opcode::Pop,
    67→            0x15 => Opcode::Push,
    68→            0x16 => Opcode::Sb,
    69→            0x17 => Opcode::Shl,
    70→            0x18 => Opcode::Sra,
    71→            0x19 => Opcode::Srl,
    72→            0x1A => Opcode::Sub,
    73→            0x1B => Opcode::SubSp,
    74→            0x1C => Opcode::Sw,
    75→            0x1D => Opcode::Sxt,
    76→            0x1E => Opcode::Xor,
    77→            0x1F => Opcode::Zxt,
    78→            _ => Opcode::Invalid,
    79→        }
    80→    }
    81→}
    82→
    83→/// Decoded instruction with opcode and register operands
    84→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    85→pub struct DecodedInstruction {
    86→    pub opcode: Opcode,
    87→    pub ra: u8,
    88→    pub rb: u8,
    89→}
    90→
    91→impl DecodedInstruction {
    92→    pub fn new(opcode: Opcode, ra: u8, rb: u8) -> Self {
    93→        Self {
    94→            opcode,
    95→            ra: ra & 0x07,
    96→            rb: rb & 0x07,
    97→        }
    98→    }
    99→
   100→    /// Decode from 12-bit ROM output
   101→    pub fn from_decoded(decoded: u16) -> Self {
   102→        let opcode = Opcode::from(((decoded >> 6) & 0x1F) as u8);
   103→        let ra = ((decoded >> 3) & 0x07) as u8;
   104→        let rb = (decoded & 0x07) as u8;
   105→        Self { opcode, ra, rb }
   106→    }
   107→}
   108→
   109→/// Instruction format type
   110→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
   111→pub enum InstructionFormat {
   112→    /// Single byte: opcode + registers encoded
   113→    SingleByte,
   114→    /// Two bytes: first byte + 8-bit immediate/offset
   115→    TwoBytes,
   116→    /// Four bytes: first byte + 24-bit address
   117→    FourBytes,
   118→}
   119→
   120→impl InstructionFormat {
   121→    /// Size in bytes
   122→    pub fn size(&self) -> usize {
   123→        match self {
   124→            InstructionFormat::SingleByte => 1,
   125→            InstructionFormat::TwoBytes => 2,
   126→            InstructionFormat::FourBytes => 4,
   127→        }
   128→    }
   129→}
   130→
   131→impl Opcode {
   132→    /// Get the instruction format for this opcode
   133→    pub fn format(&self) -> InstructionFormat {
   134→        match self {
   135→            Opcode::AddReg
   136→            | Opcode::And
   137→            | Opcode::Ceq
   138→            | Opcode::Cls
   139→            | Opcode::Clu
   140→            | Opcode::Jal
   141→            | Opcode::Jmp
   142→            | Opcode::Mov
   143→            | Opcode::Mul
   144→            | Opcode::Or
   145→            | Opcode::Pop
   146→            | Opcode::Push
   147→            | Opcode::Shl
   148→            | Opcode::Sra
   149→            | Opcode::Srl
   150→            | Opcode::Sub

## Summary

I've examined both `/Users/mike/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tf24a/asm/forth-bootstrap.s` and the reference `/Users/mike/github/sw-vibe-coding/tf24a/forth.s`. They are **identical** - the web-tf24a version is indeed a direct copy of the reference.

### What the Forth Kernel Does

This is a **COR24 DTC (Direct Threaded Code) Forth interpreter** - a complete Phase 1 bootstrap and Phase 2 threaded execution system written in COR24 assembly.

#### Expected Output

The kernel produces the following output sequence:

1. **"OK\n"** - From Phase 1 inline tests that verify data stack and UART functionality
2. **"*\n"** - From the TEST colon word (ASCII 42 is `*`)
3. **"6\n"** - From the MAIN colon word (3 doubled = 6, plus 48 = 54 = ASCII `6`)
4. Then enters an **infinite HALT loop** (at do_halt: `bra halt_loop`)

#### Architecture Highlights

**Phase 1 (lines 34-102):** Inline bootstrap code that:
- Tests data stack operations (push/pop) via UART by printing "OK\n"
- Tests return stack operations by pushing 42, popping it, and emitting it as "*"
- Prints a newline
- Exits to Phase 2

**Phase 2 (lines 104-111):** Launches threaded code execution via the `test_thread`

#### Primitives Implemented (50+ instructions)

The kernel includes a comprehensive Forth primitive library:

**I/O Primitives:**
- `do_emit` - Write character to UART with TX busy-wait
- `do_key` - Read character from UART with RX busy-wait

**Control Flow:**
- `do_exit` - End colon definition (pop IP from return stack)
- `do_lit` - Push inline literal
- `do_branch` - Unconditional branch
- `do_zbranch` - Conditional branch (0BRANCH)
- `do_halt` - Infinite halt loop

**Arithmetic:** `do_plus`, `do_minus`, `do_and`, `do_or`, `do_xor`, `do_equal`, `do_less`, `do_zequ`

**Stack Operations:** `do_drop`, `do_dup`, `do_swap`, `do_over`, `do_tor` (>R), `do_rfrom` (R>), `do_rfetch` (R@)

**Memory:** `do_fetch` (@), `do_store` (!), `do_cfetch` (C@), `do_cstore` (C!)

**Infrastructure:**
- `do_docol` - Shared entry point for all colon definitions
- Test colon words: `test_word`, `double_word`, `main_word`

#### No HALT String Output

There is **no "HALT" string printed** - the HALT primitive is simply `bra halt_loop`, an infinite loop with no output.

#### File Locations

- **Web version:** `/Users/mike/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tf24a/asm/forth-bootstrap.s`
- **Reference:** `/Users/mike/github/sw-vibe-coding/tf24a/forth.s`

input.title: cor24-emulator Public API Reference
input.text:
## EmulatorCore Public API (from /Users/mike/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs/src/emulator.rs)

### Loading & Reset
- `new() -> Self` - Create new emulator
- `load_lgo(&mut self, content: &str, entry: Option<u32>) -> Result<usize, String>` - Load LGO file
- `load_program(&mut self, addr: u32, bytes: &[u8])` - Load raw bytes at address
- `load_program_extent(&mut self, end: u32)` - Track program end address
- `reset(&mut self)` - Reset CPU (preserves memory)
- `hard_reset(&mut self)` - Clear memory and reset

### Execution Control
- `run_batch(&mut self, max_instructions: u64) -> BatchResult` - Run up to N instructions
- `step(&mut self) -> BatchResult` - Single-step with automatic re-pause
- `step_over(&mut self) -> BatchResult` - Step over calls (with temporary breakpoint at return)
- `resume(&mut self)` - Start/unpause execution
- `pause(&mut self)` - Pause execution
- `is_paused(&self) -> bool` - Check if paused
- `is_halted(&self) -> bool` - Check if CPU halted
- `is_interrupt_in_service(&self) -> bool` - Check INTIS flag
- `is_running(&self) -> bool` - Check if !paused && !halted

### Breakpoints
- `add_breakpoint(&mut self, addr: u32) -> bool` - Add breakpoint (returns true if new)
- `remove_breakpoint(&mut self, addr: u32) -> bool` - Remove by address
- `remove_breakpoint_by_index(&mut self, index: usize) -> Option<u32>` - Remove by index
- `clear_breakpoints(&mut self)` - Remove all
- `breakpoints(&self) -> &[u32]` - Get slice of all breakpoints
- `has_breakpoint(&self, addr: u32) -> bool` - Check if set

### State Queries
- `snapshot(&self) -> CpuSnapshot` - Get full CPU snapshot
- `pc(&self) -> u32` - Get program counter
- `set_pc(&mut self, pc: u32)` - Set program counter
- `get_reg(&self, reg: u8) -> u32` - Get 24-bit register (masked)
- `set_reg(&mut self, reg: u8, value: u32)` - Set 24-bit register
- `condition_flag(&self) -> bool` - Get C flag
- `cycles(&self) -> u64` - Get cycle count
- `instructions_count(&self) -> u64` - Get instruction count
- `program_end(&self) -> u32` - Get highest address written during load

### Memory Access
- `read_byte(&self, addr: u32) -> u8` - Read 8-bit at address
- `write_byte(&mut self, addr: u32, value: u8)` - Write 8-bit
- `read_word(&self, addr: u32) -> u32` - Read 24-bit (little-endian)
- `read_memory(&self, addr: u32, len: u32) -> Vec<u8>` - Read N bytes
- `sram(&self) -> &[u8]` - Direct read-only access to SRAM (0x000000-0x0FFFFF)
- `ebr(&self) -> &[u8]` - Direct read-only access to EBR (0xFEE000-0xFEFFFF)

### I/O: LED and Button
- `get_led(&self) -> u8` - Get LED state (bit 0 = D2)
- `get_button(&self) -> bool` - Get button state (true = pressed)
- `set_button_pressed(&mut self, pressed: bool)` - Set button state

### I/O: UART
- `get_uart_output(&self) -> &str` - Get accumulated UART TX output
- `clear_uart_output(&mut self)` - Clear UART output buffer
- `send_uart_byte(&mut self, byte: u8)` - Send byte to UART RX (simulates input)
- `set_uart_tx_busy_cycles(&mut self, cycles: u8)` - Set TX busy duration (0=instant)
- `set_uart_never_ready(&mut self, never_ready: bool)` - For testing poll-before-write

### Disassembly
- `disassemble_at(&self, addr: u32) -> (String, u32)` - Disassemble one instruction at address, returns (mnemonic, size_in_bytes)
- `disassemble(&self, addr: u32, count: usize) -> Vec<(u32, String, u32)>` - Disassemble N instructions, returns vec of (pc, mnemonic, size)

### Trace
- `trace(&self) -> &crate::cpu::state::TraceBuffer` - Get instruction trace buffer

## Supporting Types

### BatchResult
```rust
pub struct BatchResult {
    pub instructions_run: u64,
    pub reason: StopReason,
    pub uart_bytes_added: usize,
    pub led_changed: bool,
}
```

### StopReason
```rust
pub enum StopReason {
    CycleLimit,                  // Ran requested # of instructions
    Breakpoint(u32),             // Hit breakpoint at address
    Halted,                      // CPU halted
    InvalidInstruction(u8),      // Invalid instruction byte
    Paused,                       // Emulator is paused
}
```

### CpuSnapshot
```rust
pub struct CpuSnapshot {
    pub regs: [u32; 8],          // r0-r7 (all masked to 24-bit)
    pub pc: u32,
    pub c: bool,                 // Condition flag
    pub halted: bool,
    pub intis: bool,             // Interrupt in service
    pub cycles: u64,
    pub instructions: u64,
    pub led: u8,
    pub button: u8,
    pub uart_output_len: usize,
}
```

### CpuState I/O Fields (accessed via write_byte at addresses)
Memory-mapped I/O:
- `0xFF0000` IO_LEDSWDAT - LED/Switch: write bit 0 for LED D2, read bit 0 for button S2
- `0xFF0010` IO_INTENABLE - Interrupt enable: bit 0 = UART RX interrupt
- `0xFF0100` IO_UARTDATA - UART data: write to transmit, read to receive (auto-ack RX)
- `0xFF0101` IO_UARTSTAT - UART status:
  - bit 0: RX data ready
  - bit 1: CTS active
  - bit 2: RX overflow
  - bit 7: TX busy

### ISA Types (cor24-isa crate at ../../sw-embed/cor24-rs/isa/)

#### Opcode enum
32 opcodes from AddReg (0x00) to Zxt (0x1F), plus Invalid (0xFF)

#### Opcode methods
- `opcode.format(&self) -> InstructionFormat` - SingleByte, TwoBytes, or FourBytes
- `opcode.mnemonic(&self) -> &'static str` - e.g., "add", "lc", "jal"

#### InstructionFormat
- `SingleByte` - 1 byte opcode+registers
- `TwoBytes` - opcode + 8-bit immediate/offset
- `FourBytes` - opcode + 24-bit immediate

#### DecodedInstruction
```rust
pub struct DecodedInstruction {
    pub opcode: Opcode,
    pub ra: u8,      // 3-bit register A
    pub rb: u8,      // 3-bit register B
}
```
- `from_decoded(decoded: u16) -> Self` - Decode from 12-bit ROM output [opcode(5):ra(3):rb(3)]

#### Register names (cor24-isa::register)
```
["r0", "r1", "r2", "fp", "sp", "z", "r6", "r7"]
 0     1     2     3      4     5    6     7
```
- `reg_name(reg: u8) -> &'static str` - Get name for 3-bit index
- `parse_register(s: &str) -> Option<u8>` - Parse name to index

## UART Poll-Before-Feed Pattern (from web-tml24c/src/repl.rs)

web-tml24c shows reference pattern for browser UI:
1. Maintain `uart_tx_queue: VecDeque<u8>` for pending input
2. In each Tick message:
   - Before feeding queued bytes, check UART status: `cpu.read_byte(0xFF0101) & 0x01`
   - If status & 0x01 != 0 (TX busy), run_batch(50) to let CPU process
   - When status & 0x01 == 0 (TX ready), pop byte and send_uart_byte()
   - Continue with limited feed_budget to avoid blocking browser
3. On each Tick, get UART output with `get_uart_output()` and track `uart_seen` index for new bytes
4. Auto-scroll when new output appears

Register COR24 allocation (from tf24a):
- r0 = W (work/scratch)
- r1 = RSP (return stack pointer, ~0x0F0000 growing down)
- r2 = IP (instruction pointer for threaded code)
- sp (r4) = DSP (data stack, hardware EBR)
- fp (r3) = available as extra scratch
- Cell size = 3 bytes (24-bit words)
- Data stack grows downward from initial_sp
- EBR: 0xFEE000-0xFEEC00 (3KB populated)

## cor24-emulator Public API Reference

### Core Types & Structure

**EmulatorCore** (`/Users/mike/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs/src/emulator.rs`) provides the main interface:

#### Loading & Reset
- `new() -> Self`
- `load_lgo(&mut self, content: &str, entry: Option<u32>) -> Result<usize, String>`
- `load_program(&mut self, addr: u32, bytes: &[u8])`
- `load_program_extent(&mut self, end: u32)`
- `reset(&mut self)` and `hard_reset(&mut self)`

#### Execution Control
- `run_batch(&mut self, max_instructions: u64) -> BatchResult`
- `step(&mut self) -> BatchResult` — single-step with auto re-pause
- `step_over(&mut self) -> BatchResult` — steps over calls using temporary breakpoint
- `resume(&mut self)` and `pause(&mut self)`
- `is_paused()`, `is_halted()`, `is_running()`, `is_interrupt_in_service()`

#### Breakpoints
- `add_breakpoint(&mut self, addr: u32) -> bool`
- `remove_breakpoint(&mut self, addr: u32) -> bool`
- `clear_breakpoints(&mut self)`
- `breakpoints(&self) -> &[u32]`
- `has_breakpoint(&self, addr: u32) -> bool`

#### State Queries
- `snapshot(&self) -> CpuSnapshot` — returns full CPU state (regs, pc, c flag, cycles, instructions, led, button, uart_output_len)
- `pc()`, `set_pc()`, `get_reg(reg: u8)`, `set_reg(reg: u8, value: u32)`
- `condition_flag()`, `cycles()`, `instructions_count()`, `program_end()`

#### Memory Access
- `read_byte(addr: u32) -> u8` and `write_byte(addr: u32, value: u8)`
- `read_word(addr: u32) -> u32` — 24-bit little-endian
- `read_memory(addr: u32, len: u32) -> Vec<u8>`
- `sram() -> &[u8]` — direct SRAM access (0x000000-0x0FFFFF)
- `ebr() -> &[u8]` — direct EBR access (0xFEE000-0xFEFFFF)

#### I/O: LED & Button
- `get_led() -> u8` (bit 0 = LED D2)
- `get_button() -> bool` (true = pressed)
- `set_button_pressed(&mut self, pressed: bool)`

#### I/O: UART
- `get_uart_output(&self) -> &str` — accumulated TX output
- `clear_uart_output(&mut self)`
- `send_uart_byte(&mut self, byte: u8)` — inject RX byte

- `set_uart_never_ready(&mut self, never_ready: bool)` — for testing poll-before-write

#### Disassembly
- `disassemble_at(&self, addr: u32) -> (String, u32)` — returns (mnemonic, size_in_bytes)
- `disassemble(&self, addr: u32, count: usize) -> Vec<(u32, String, u32)>` — returns vec of (pc, mnemonic, size)

```rust
pub struct BatchResult {
    pub instructions_run: u64,
    pub reason: StopReason,
    pub uart_bytes_added: usize,
    pub led_changed: bool,
}
```

- `Breakpoint(u32)` — hit breakpoint
- `Halted` — CPU halted
- `InvalidInstruction(u8)` — invalid opcode
- `Paused` — manually paused

file.content:
; forth.s — tf24a DTC Forth: Phase 1 (bootstrap) + Phase 2 (threaded code)
; COR24 DTC Forth kernel
;
; Register allocation (frozen):
;   r0 = W (work/scratch)
;   r1 = RSP (return stack pointer, grows down from 0x0F0000)
;   r2 = IP (instruction pointer for threaded code)
;   sp = DSP (data stack, hardware push/pop in EBR)
;   fp = limited scratch (many instructions don't support fp)
;
; UART: data at 0xFF0100 (-65280), status at 0xFF0101 (-65279)
;   TX busy = status bit 7, RX ready = status bit 0
;
; DTC NEXT (inlined at tail of every primitive):
;   lw r0, 0(r2)    ; W = mem[IP] — fetch code address from thread
;   add r2, 3       ; IP += cell
;   jmp (r0)        ; execute code
;
; Colon word layout (3 bytes CFA):
;   word_entry:          ; thread entries point here
;       bra do_docol     ; 2 bytes — branch to shared DOCOL
;       .byte 0          ; 1 byte padding (never executed)
;   word_pfa:            ; PFA = CFA + 3
;       .word do_xxx     ; parameter field (thread of word addresses)
;       ...
;       .word do_exit

; ============================================================
; Entry point (address 0)
; ============================================================
_start:
    la r1, 983040       ; r1 = 0x0F0000 return stack base

    ; ============================================================
    ; Phase 1: Inline Tests — print "OK\n*\n"
    ; ============================================================

    ; Test 1: Data stack + UART — print "OK\n"
    lc r0, 10           ; '\n'
    push r0
    lc r0, 75           ; 'K'
    push r0
    lc r0, 79           ; 'O'
    push r0

    la r2, -65280       ; r2 = UART base (IP not needed yet)

    ; Emit 'O'
    pop r0
    push r0
tx1:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt tx1
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)

    ; Emit 'K'
    pop r0
    push r0
tx2:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt tx2
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)

    ; Emit '\n'
    pop r0
    push r0
tx3:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt tx3
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)

    ; Test 2: Return stack — push 42, clear, pop, emit '*'
    lc r0, 42
    add r1, -3
    sw r0, 0(r1)
    lc r0, 0
    lw r0, 0(r1)
    add r1, 3

    push r0
tx4:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt tx4
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)

    ; Emit '\n'
    lc r0, 10
    push r0
tx5:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt tx5
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)

    ; ============================================================
    ; Phase 2: Launch threaded code test
    ; ============================================================
    la r2, test_thread  ; IP = start of test thread
    ; NEXT — bootstrap into threaded execution
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; Primitives
; ============================================================

; ------------------------------------------------------------
; EMIT ( c -- ) : Write character to UART with TX busy-wait
; ------------------------------------------------------------
do_emit:

; ============================================================
; Test Colon Definitions (Phase 2)
; ============================================================

; : TEST  42 EMIT 10 EMIT ;   — prints "*\n"
test_word:
    bra do_docol
    .byte 0
    .word do_lit
    .word 42
    .word do_emit
    .word do_lit
    .word 10
    .word do_emit
    .word do_exit

; : DOUBLE  DUP + ;
double_word:
    bra do_docol
    .byte 0
    .word do_dup
    .word do_plus
    .word do_exit

; : MAIN  3 DOUBLE 48 + EMIT 10 EMIT ;   — prints "6\n"
; 3 DOUBLE → 6, then 6 + 48 = 54 = ASCII '6'
main_word:
    bra do_docol
    .byte 0
    .word do_lit
    .word 3
    .word double_word
    .word do_lit
    .word 48
    .word do_plus
    .word do_emit
    .word do_lit
    .word 10
    .word do_emit
    .word do_exit

; ============================================================
; Top-level test thread (entered from Phase 1 bootstrap)
; Runs MAIN (prints "6\n"), then TEST (prints "*\n"), then halts.
; ============================================================
test_thread:
    .word main_word
    .word test_word
    .word do_halt
